articles.title: ECB Schnabel: There is no need to rush into action
articles.expand: ECB's Schnabel is speaking and says: We are facing a massive energy price shock. There is no need to rush into action. We have time to analyze the data. ECB should not be rushed to raise rates. articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: War headlines and US employment to set the tone
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair ends the week around 1.1530, barely below its opening but off the peak at 1.1640. Widespread optimism on Monday was diluted as days went by, and financial markets moved once again on war-related headlines.

articles.title: Canada: Growth outlook steady with partial rebound – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) economists Claire Fan and Abbey Xu expect Canadian GDP to be essentially flat in January after December’s 0.2% gain, with weakness concentrated in autos and housing but offset by energy and retail.

articles.title: Euro area: War-driven energy shock reshapes ECB path – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Dr. Vincent Stamer updates Euro area inflation projections incorporating the Iran War and associated energy shock. The bank expects higher Oil and natural gas prices to push headline inflation above 3% in 2026 before easing back to the ECB’s 2% target by 2027.
